Demolition Labor
costItems.description:
Removal and disposal of 4 feet of existing drywall panels from the walls.
Inspection of framing and insulation for moisture damage

costItems.name: Substrate preparation labor
costItems.description:
Installation of vapor barrier or moisture membrane (if not already present or if damaged)
Treatment of framing with anti-microbial solution (if necessary)

costItems.name: Cement baker board labor
costItems.description:
Installation of cementitious backer board panels
Proper fastening with corrosion-resistant screws
Sealing of joints using alkali-resistant fiberglass mesh tape
Application of thin-set mortar or an appropriate joint compound for cement board
Install an inside-wall access valve with a protective plate to prevent water from entering the wall.

costItems.name: Waterproofing labor
costItems.description:
Application of a liquid waterproofing membrane over cement board
This additional waterproofing layer provides enhanced protection against vapor migration and water intrusion.

costItems.name: Concrete evaluation labor
costItems.description: Moisture levels must be checked — excessive moisture can cause blistering and delamination

costItems.name: Cleaning labor
costItems.description:
Remove dust, oil, grease, chemical residues, and contaminants.
Use appropriate degreasers if necessary

Rinse thoroughly and allow complete drying

costItems.name: Mechanical surface preparation labor
costItems.description:
Perform heavy grinding, diamond grinding, or shot blasting.
Objective: open the concrete pores and create a surface profile for mechanical bonding.
The surface should have a rough texture similar to coarse sandpaper

costItems.name: Repair labor
costItems.description:
Cracks must be opened and treated with epoxy filler or repair mortar.
Holes and surface defects must be leveled

costItems.name: Application of epoxy labor
costItems.description:
Apply a primer compatible with the selected system.
The primer penetrates the pores and promotes chemical bonding between the concrete and coating

costItems.name: Application of epoxy labor
costItems.description:
Apply according to the specified thickness (typically 2 to 4 mm for technical areas).
For severe chemical environments, a 2-coat system is recommended.

costItems.name: Disconnect
costItems.description:
Shut down the system
Drain tank and piping
Disconnect the pump and filter
Cut and cap supply/return lines
Remove tank and equipment (leave onsite)

costItems.name: Reconnect
costItems.description:
Reset the tank on the stand
Reinstall the pump and filter
Reconnect all PVC piping
Replace unions, fittings, and valves as required
Pressure test and leak check
Startup and operational check

costItems.name: Plumbing
costItems.description:
1-Replace the emergency shutoff valve for your plumbing fixture. 
2-Lower kitchen sink drain
3-Install a vacuum breaker
4-Strap water lines
5-Pressure test water lines
6-Install tub support
7-Install a customer supplied toilet. Includes any fastening or
sealing, as well as thorough testing to ensure proper installation.
8-Install plate - handle and shower head for shower and tub trim
9-Install a customer supplied lav or bar faucet installation including
a thorough check of all shutoffs under the sink, as well as
connecting water supply lines and drainage.
10-Install a customer supplied kitchen or laundry faucet including a
thorough check of all shutoffs under the sink, as well as
connecting water supply lines.
11-Install a customer supplied dishwasher by connecting the
dishwasher to incoming water and outgoing drainage, as well as a
thorough testing to ensure proper installation.
12-Install a mechanical air vent device that will allow for easy drain
cleaning access and also improve your drain's performance.
13- Set toilet flange

costItems.name: Synthetic Shingle Roof Replacement
costItems.description:
• Pull required roofing permits with the local building department as
As well as file NOC with the county clerk.
• Tear off the existing roof down to the decking.
• Inspect and replace any damaged or rotten wood and flashing (Please see our
pricelist) WOOD Included: 3 Plywood
• Re-nail decking to bring it up to code
• Install Peel and Stick Underlayment only on vulnerable roof areas such as
as eaves, valleys, and roof-to-wall connections. Install Synthetic
underlayment as the primary underlayment.
• Install shingle starter along the eaves
• Install Drip edge with the color of your choosing
• Install architectural shingles: GAF SHINGLES
• Install and color-match plumbing boots and vents
• Install hip and ridge cap shingles
• Install Ridge ventilation system
• Clean up the roof, gutters, and the surrounding area
• Run magnets to ensure no nails are left in the ground
• Haul off all roofing debris
• 5-year workmanship warranty

> Work will be done by Subcontractor: All American Enterprises - CCCB13760 / CGC 57508

costItems.name: Project Breakdown - Framing, Drywall and Painting
costItems.description:
Framing and Door Installation:
- Frame wall extension over existing wall with dimensions 196" L x 54" H (74 sq ft)
- Frame new wall measuring 95" x 117"
- Create rough opening for 36" x 80" door in the 95" x 117" wall
- Install prehung door in the rough opening
- Ensure all framing is plumb, level, and square
- Install appropriate headers and structural support as required
- Coordinate with other trades for any utility rough-ins before drywall installation

Drywall Installation and Finishing:
- Install drywall on both sides of the wall extension (196" L x 54" H - approximately 148 sq ft total)
- Install drywall on both sides of the new framed wall (95" x 117" - approximately 218 sq ft total)
- Cut drywall around door opening and trim as needed
- Apply joint compound (mud) to all seams and screw holes
- Sand all mudded areas smooth and prepare surfaces for painting
- Prime all new drywall surfaces
- Ensure all corners are properly finished with corner bead where applicable

Painting:
- Paint both sides of the wall extension (196" L x 54" H) with standard wall paint color
- Paint both sides of the new wall (95" x 117") in black color as specified
- Paint door trim and casing around the 36" x 80" door opening
- Apply appropriate primer where needed
- Ensure clean, professional finish with proper coverage
- Protect surrounding areas and flooring during painting operations

Notes:
1- Frame wall extension over existing wall, dimensions 196"L x 54" (74 sq ft), install drywall both sides, mud, sand, paint.
2- Frame wall 95" x 117", rough opening for 36" x 80", install prehung door, install drywall both sides, paint in black.

costItems.name: SHOWER REMODELING
costItems.description:
1) DEMOLITION PREPARATION:

Remove existing plastic shower walls and floor

Remove existing shower drain fixture

Remove existing vanity cabinet

Haul away all demolition debris

Clean and prepare shower area for new construction

Prepare vanity area for new installation

2) PLUMBING WORK:

Install new shower drain with proper slope and connection to existing drain line

Install new shower valve rough-in with hot and cold water lines

Connect new shower head supply line

Install new vanity faucet with hot and cold water supply lines

Install drain line for new vanity

Test all water connections for proper flow and no leaks

Ensure all plumbing meets Florida building codes

3) FRAMING AND SUBSTRATE PREPARATION:

Frame or reinforce shower walls as needed for proper slope and structure

Install blocking for shower valve and accessories

Prepare substrate for cement board installation

Ensure all framing is square and level

Floor Leveling and Base Preparation:

Level shower floor area measuring 40 square feet using cement

Create proper slope toward drain for water drainage

Allow cement to cure properly before proceeding with next phase

Ensure floor is smooth and ready for shower pan liner installation

4) CEMENT BOARD INSTALLATION:

Install cement board on shower walls covering 140 square feet of tile area

Install cement board on shower floor measuring 40 square feet

Secure cement board with corrosion-resistant fasteners

Tape and seal all cement board joints with waterproof joint compound

Ensure all surfaces are flat and properly secured

5) WATERPROOFING APPLICATION:

Apply waterproofing membrane to all cement board surfaces in shower area

Install shower pan liner on floor measuring 40 square feet

Ensure waterproofing covers all seams and penetrations

Allow waterproofing to cure per manufacturer specifications

Waterproof all areas where tile will be installed

6) TILE INSTALLATION AND GROUTING:

Install customer-supplied tile on shower walls covering 140 square feet

Install customer-supplied tile on shower floor measuring 40 square feet

Set tile with proper thinset mortar and spacing

Install tile around shower drain and valve penetrations

Apply grout to all tile joints

Clean excess grout and finish grout lines

Seal grout as needed per manufacturer recommendations

Ensure all tile is level and properly adhered

7) SHOWER GLASS DOOR INSTALLATION:

Install customer-supplied shower glass door

Secure door frame to shower walls with proper fasteners

Install door hinges and hardware

Ensure door operates smoothly and seals properly

Install any required seals or gaskets

8)VANITY INSTALLATION:

Install customer-supplied vanity cabinet in proper location

Secure vanity to wall studs

Ensure vanity is level and plumb

Prepare vanity top for faucet installation

Vanity Faucet Installation:

Install customer-supplied faucet on vanity

Connect faucet to hot and cold water supply lines

Connect drain line to vanity drain

Test faucet for proper operation and no leaks

- MATERIAL: TILE TO FLOOR AND WALLS, SHOWER DOOR, SHOWER HEAD, AND VALVES, AND OTHER FINISHES CUSTOMER SUPPLIES.
